notes:
The native version of this game runs horrible with many graphical limitations and game play breaking glitches.

The proton version works much better, it can be played in full with its graphical options maxed out and after some initial stuttering as it builds its Proton cache will be playable completely smooth.

I rank this Silver because Battleye is not Linux compatible, this means that while you get the best experience on the Proton version you will not be able to join BattlEye protected servers.

So completely playable with friends on an unprotected private server but not suitable for Online play.
title: ARK: Survival Evolved

notes: With proton running windows version the game loads much more faster, runs faster and looks much better. With old under-powered Nvidia 750ti I can feel the difference from native version, the smoothness is noticeable. The only issue is this game requires BattleEye and that doesn't works on proton, so no multiplayer gaming for servers that require it.
title: ARK: Survival Evolved

notes: Doesn't supports mods. Not playable on online servers because battle eye doesn't works and not even unofficial servers without battle eye because most of them have mods.
title: ARK: Survival Evolved

notes: Sometimes there are issues in connecting to servers or refreshing the server list. How ever I did not tested playing on Battle Eye Servers (Only on my own with out Battle Eye)
title: ARK: Survival Evolved

notes: Supports Mods, so it is possible to play on unofficial servers without battleeye! Just make sure to uninstall any previous Linux mods by unsuscribing from them in the workshop, removing all files in ~/.steam/steam/SteamApps/common/ARK/ShooterGame/Content/Mods/ and ~/.steam/steam/SteamApps/workshop/content/346110/
title: ARK: Survival Evolved

notes:
Native version of this game is seriously underdeveloped, runs awful and has basically nothing but the basic graphics of the Windows version (e.g. there is no ground foliage).

Luckily running the Windows version through proton seems to work great other than the occasional stuttering from dynamic LOD being a bit slow. I believe was an issue in the original game anyhow it is just more noticeable as it has to now go through the proton and vulcan API layer.
title: ARK: Survival Evolved

notes: I tried the game with proton 4.3-2. It works very well in a single player. I have not tried the online mode. The performance is excellent compared to its "native version".
title: ARK: Survival Evolved

notes: After getting nowhere with WD, I decided to try Ark out using the windows version with Proton. After turning off Battle eye, it runs really well. I can see grass and snow again. I just found out I can tweak the setting, so I will try that next. Until they fix the "native" version, I am using 4.2-9 ( I thought I was using 4.11. I think my fps are actually better than playing it in windows (Dual boot)
title: ARK: Survival Evolved

notes: The native Linux version of ARK has a few graphical bugs and is generally speaking lacking in features compared to the Windows version, but otherwise pretty stable and not bad performance wise either. If you can run the Windows version, it's advised to do so, but unfortunately BattlEye is not supported on Proton, so the best report I can give this game running in Proton is Silver. However if you're not playing on official server, this is often not an issue, a dedicated server among friends doesn't need BattlEye and it can be disabled. I did not find I needed any tweaks to get the game to run. The performance isn't great, but the game is simply poorly optimised, that's not really a Proton issue. Only minor issue I encountered was with changing graphical settings, I found every time I changed them the fps would get worse until the game slowed to a crawl, but restarting the game fixed that. If you need to change the settings, ANY setting at all, do a restart after to check performance. Once you've found a nice 'sweet spot' for graphical settings, this shouldn't be an issue.
title: ARK: Survival Evolved

cpu: AMD Ryzen 3 2200G with Radeon Vega Graphics

title: ARK: Survival Evolved

notes: Today i tested the game with proton 4.11-6 after weeks with proton 4.9-2. It seems to be it runs much better than before, smoother and the rx 480 don´t get so hot. As someone wrote the game is "simply poorly optimised". No crashes on the valguero map.
title: ARK: Survival Evolved
